_voted_data_note: No Knesset voting data available. Bennett did not serve in the 25th Knesset (retired from politics June 2022, returned April 2025). His listed candidates have no relevant Knesset voting record. Positions are based on Bennett's record as PM (2021-2022), party platform, and public statements.

stated_position.source: As PM, tolerated settlement blocs but opposed unauthorized outposts; security-driven pragmatism over ideological expansion
'Shrinking the conflict' approach — neither formal two-state advocate nor annexationist; left final status deliberately open
Supports military operations with clear strategic goals; opposed open-ended indefinite campaign
Jerusalem as undivided capital — consistent position
Opposes right of return
Opposed annexation as PM — froze Smotrich annexation moves; pragmatic security-first approach
Universal service (equal burden) is a signature Bennett theme — tried to advance conscription legislation as PM
Supports civil marriage alternatives; advanced spousal registration as PM
Secular lifestyle rights; supported Shabbat commerce liberalization
Supported Kotel compromise framework as PM
Opposed unconditional yeshiva funding; linked funding to national service participation
Opposed state kashrut monopoly; supported reform of rabbinate control
Center-right on healthcare; moderate spending increases
Supported housing programs as PM; worked with Interior Ministry on approvals
Pragmatic — not a strong position either way
Mixed record; pragmatic rather than ideological on state ownership
Tech-sector background; supports market competition as cost-of-living driver
Strongly and publicly opposes override clause; made this a central campaign message for 2026
Opposed politicization of judiciary appointments
Respected AG authority as PM; abided by AG opinions even when inconvenient
Supports courts retaining reasonableness review power
Judicial independence as essential safeguard — explicitly stated in 2025-2026 campaign
Supports equality for all citizens; worked with Ra'am as coalition partner
Led coalition that included Ra'am (2021-2022) — demonstrated willingness to govern with Arab parties
Committed to multi-year economic plan for Arab sector as PM
Did not actively oppose Nation-State Law; pragmatic on Jewish state definition
Supports PM accountability; calls for Netanyahu to step down during trial
Supports term limits for PM
Neutral — new party unconstrained by threshold history
Supports campaign finance transparency and limits
Bennett calls for full accountability; supports state inquiry as former PM who set up intelligence frameworks
Bennett strongly promoted core curriculum as PM and as 2026 campaign theme: 'every child deserves an education'
Supports media standards; has spoken about media reform and accountability
